rulings.Case Title: IN RE: THE JACQUELINE WARD 2007 REVOCABLE TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #5, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 7/14/21. (No earlier availability.) Issues necessitating continuance: Proof of the additional bond ordered on 5/6/21 has not yet been filed. Whether or not the Judicial Council forms are used by a standard account filer, a subsequent account must state the date of any order authorizing payment of fiduciary or attorney fees. (It would have been helpful if the schedule of assets on hand at the end of the prior account had been the first schedule in the accounting, not the last. Is the market value of the real property really still the same as the carry value?) All petitions involving a trust must state the names and last known addresses of all vested or contingent beneficiaries. [CRC 7.902.] Was there a prior order authorizing the successor trustee to pay the conservator for a portion of her mortgage and other costs associated with her home? The cash withdrawal for "personal allowance" in January 2019 far exceeded the previous amounts. The date of the order authorizing an allowance is not set forth in the accounting. The ex parte order filed on 11/7/19, increasing the monthly amount, did not authorize the amount shown in the accounting. Reimbursements to any individual must specify who the original payee was. Please file receipts for the cash withdrawals made on 7/19/19, 9/3/19, 12/10/19, and 6/19/20 for clothing. Disbursements for gifts with vague descriptions may also need to be supported (two wedding gifts of cash four days apart?). Why were there two cash withdrawals in January 2019 for payments to the unidentified hairdresser? What was the date of the order authorizing payments to caregivers prior to 11/7/19 and why were there periodic cash withdrawals in addition? Other disbursements to Wal-Mart, Sears, and Office Depot for "household" without more and a $1,500 cash withdrawal on 9/14/18 for "rehabilitation" without more are not sufficient. Receipts may need to be filed. When was the balance of the "emergency loan" taken by the trustee repaid? Why was there an additional "transfer fee for advance" to the trustee of $35 on 10/4/18? The attorney for the conservator did not verify this petition as to her request for fees. The conservator has not yet filed a notice of change of her address in the conservatorship case, as noted by the Court Investigator. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 6/30/21. After these issues are addressed, an appearance may be required in connection with the fee request for the prior attorney. (Only the account statements showing the account balance as of the closing date of the accounting period of the court accounting need to be filed in interim accountings. [Probate Code Section 2620(c)(2). Ruth Sullivan was discharged on 10/1/15.) Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF VARTKES ANIVIAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 6/29/2021. 1) Please explain and provide legal authority for why interest was paid on the gift to Marie Amoudjaian when Article 11 of the Will says, "No interest shall be paid on any gift or legacy under this Will or any Codicil to it." It appears $437.84 needs to be paid back to the estate. 2) The petition for final distribution must state specifically how the estate is to be distributed to each distributee, including the net amount of cash (as of a date certain) to be distributed to each beneficiary. [LRSF 14.36 I. 6)]. It appears an amendment to the petition needs to be filed and noticed. (CRC 7.53). 3) The caption of the order must begin with Order for Final Distribution. [LRSF 14.36 I. 6)]. 4) Please explain why the proposed order does not match what is requested in the petition for example see proposed order page 3 #5, #7, #8, #9, and #10. Please submit a revised proposed order that matches what is requested in the petition. The math in the proposed order does not appear correct and proposed order does not match what was requested in the petition. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 6/14/2021 [LRSF 14.7 B.] Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF NANCY MAZZA
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 7/13/21. (No earlier availability.) Issues necessitating continuance: Original financial account statements (or original letters signed by an officer of the financial institution having the authority to sign, showing the vesting of the account, the date, and the amount of the balance) must be filed to verify the ending balance of assets. Any difference between the statement(s) and the account must be reconciled. [LRSF 14.35 D.] If all assets were community property, the surviving spouse must affirmatively state that she has been fully informed about the reasons for probate, rather than, e.g., a Spousal Property Petition, and the consequences thereof. [LRSF 14.36 I.5.] It appears that the Supplemental I&A should have been corrected, too, not just the Final I&A, as the accounting begins with a figure that differs from the I&As. (Although the Corrected I&A filed on 4/13/21 was captioned "Corrected Supplemental", the assets in that corrected I&A were cash assets, like the cash asset listed in the Final I&A filed on 12/5/19.) Paragraph 4 of the prayer is insufficient. A petition for final distribution must state specifically how the estate is to be distributed by showing the distributee's name and description of the property, including the net amount of cash (as of a date certain) distributable to each beneficiary, not just "as set forth in the" petition. [CRC 7.651; LRSF 14.36 I.6.] Assets that were not subject to probate in this proceeding do not need to be mentioned. The Court will not make any ruling or order regarding such assets not marshaled and inventoried in the estate. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 6/30/21. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B. (Rev. & Tax. Code 19513 was repealed. The objections have not yet been fully reviewed.)

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ROBERT ZELMAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 7/13/21. (No earlier availability.) Issues necessitating continuance: Original financial statements must be filed to verify the cash on hand. [LRSF 14.35 D.] Whenever originals are E-filed, the attorney or the fiduciary who retains the original must file a declaration certifying that the original document(s) will be retained in the same way as documents are retained under Local Rule 2.11 M.4., which provides that the original(s) must be retained "for at least the period after the termination, including all appeals, of the case required for conventionally filed documents." There is nothing attached to the petition to confirm that this administrator has been appointed in the decedent's mother's estate. Certified Letters (or equivalent thereof) must be filed. [LRSF 14.36 G.] Akin to Probate Code Section 1208, since this administrator is reportedly also the administrator of the decedent's post-deceased mother, all parties who have an interest in the mother's estate must be given notice. If there was $155,035.72 in cash on hand as of 2/16/2021, after subtracting the statutory fee/commission, there should be $143,579.98 on hand to be distributed, not $143,129.98. The filing fee for this final petition was $435, not $450. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 6/30/21. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF VICTOR CHURCH
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Appearance required. Issues to include: 1) The will names "Victor Inglesias" as the beneficiary and executor but Petitioner spells his name "Iglesias". Petitioner alleges that there is no other person that has a similar spelled name. 2) Petitioner alleges that he is aware of bank accounts that total over $100,000 and that nothing short of Letters will allow Petitioner to take possession of those assets. 3) Petitioner is not represented by counsel. Court to determine whether he will be able to competently administer this estate. Remote appearances should be made whenever possible. Please review the 9th amended general order. The following information pertains to appearances by Zoom: WHEN ENTERING "YOUR NAME" ON ZOOM, BOTH THE FIRST AND LAST NAME, AS WELL THE CASE NUMBER, MUST BE INCLUDED. Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833, and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Information regarding appearance by CourtCall can be found in the 9th amended general order.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF SHI-MEI CHOW SHEN
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR SPOUSAL PROPERTY
rulings.Rulings: Appearance required. Issues to include: 1) Title to the real property is held as "Chia Ming-Shen and Chow Shi-Meilshen, his wife". Petitioner alleges that Decedent's maiden name in Chinese is Chow Shi-Mei (Chow being her surname). When she married, her name in Chinese became Shen Chow Shi-Mei. Petitioner states that it appears the drafter of the deed simply added Shen to Decedent's maiden name (which would have made it Chow Shi-Mei Shen) and also mistakenly added an "L" between Mei and Shen. Remote appearances should be made whenever possible. Please review the 9th amended general order. The following information pertains to appearances by Zoom: WHEN ENTERING "YOUR NAME" ON ZOOM, BOTH THE FIRST AND LAST NAME, AS WELL THE CASE NUMBER, MUST BE INCLUDED. Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833, and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Information regarding appearance by CourtCall can be found in the 9th amended general order.

Has not filed Notice of Petition to Administer Estate (DE-121). 3. The Declaration of attorney alleges JoAnn Jones has been nominated by Decedent's siblings to obtain letters but nothing has been filed showing the nomination. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 6/14/2021 [LRSF 14.7 B.] Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ROBERT KENTON KISTLER
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 7/13/21. (No earlier availability.) Issues necessitating continuance: The Acknowledgment of Receipt of the Duties and Liabilities is incomplete (no telephone number). No typewritten version of the holographic will was attached to the Proof of Holographic Instrument. [LRSF 14.18 A.] When second generation or more distant heirs are listed, the deceased ancestor through whom they take (or would have taken) shall be named and his or her date of death and relationship to the decedent shall be stated. [LRSF 14.18 C.] Who were the decedent's parents and when did they die? (The nephews are presumably the children of Heather Stapley, although item 8 does not specify.) Notice to a minor must be sent directly to the minor. A separate copy of the notice must be sent to the person or persons having legal custody of the minor, with whom the minor resides. Item 8 of the petition does not indicate who that person is. (Item 5a(3) of the petition is checked, although item 5a(1) was checked...? The will identified Bruce Beger (not Berger) as a father in law, not step-father A portion of the typewritten will attached to the petition was incomplete ("18th birthday or for colle"). A supplement to the petition, signed and verified by the petitioner, must be filed to address the issues above. A courtesy copy of supplement and complete Duties and Liabilities and complete Proof of Holographic Instrument must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 6/30/21. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.
